name: Grape-Plum Jelly
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 10 half-pints
Procedure:
1. Wash and pit the plums; do not peel them.
2. Thoroughly crush the plums and grapes, one layer at a time, in a saucepan with water.
3. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes.
4. Strain the juice through a jelly bag or double layer of cheesecloth.
5. Measure the sugar and set aside.
6. Combine 6-1/2 cups of juice with butter and pectin in a large saucepan.
7. Bring to a hard boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
8. Add the sugar and return to a full rolling boil.
9. Boil hard for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
10. Remove from heat, quickly skim off foam, and fill sterile jars, leaving 1/4-inch headspace.
11. Adjust lids and process as recommended in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Grape-Plum Jelly in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 minutes
1,001 - 6,000 ft: 10 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 15 minutes
